{"id":"207a955d-7044-4ef7-bb0a-b6b712180f56","identifier":"FRG-237","title":"Set up QA regression suite for Hey Chill","description":"## Context

Hey Chill M1 is complete. Before M2 starts, we need automated regression tests.

## What to do

1. Read the project description in Linear — it contains the cumulative feature spec.
2. Set up testing in the main repo (chillkimtest-oss/hey-chill). Playwright recommended for PWA testing.
3. Write regression tests covering: push-to-talk UI, VAD silence timeout (3.5s), transcription display, Slack webhook send, PWA installability, service worker.
4. Tests run against: [https://brainwave-ten.vercel.app](<https://brainwave-ten.vercel.app>)

## Key Constraints

* Web Speech API may not work in headless Chrome — consider how to test transcription. May need to mock the SpeechRecognition API.
* Webhook send test: verify the fetch call is made with correct payload. May need to intercept network requests rather than hit the real webhook.
* PWA tests: verify manifest, service worker registration, installability criteria.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Test suite covers all features listed in the project description
* Tests pass against current deployment
* README explains how to run tests","priority":{"value":2,"name":"High"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-237/set-up-qa-regression-suite-for-hey-chill","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-237-set-up-qa-regression-suite-for-hey-chill","createdAt":"2026-03-07T23:22:09.633Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-07T23:22:20.711Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":null,"dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Todo","labels":["testing"],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"Hey Chill","projectId":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

USERMESSAGE #7
✓ TOOL RESULT

{"id":"5c39b383-018d-4b3b-97c7-d9a5129c119d","icon":null,"color":"#bec2c8","name":"Hey Chill","summary":"Voice-first AI companion — dogfooding OpenClaw","description":"## Final Goal

Kim uses it daily for hands-free interaction with their agents.

## Approach (revised 2026-03-05)

Standalone PWA — decoupled from AIRI. Installable on phone/tablet/desktop.

* Always-on wake word (Hey Chill) OR push-to-talk button
* Voice → transcription → send to Chill via Slack (n8n webhook)
* No Live2D, no AIRI complexity — just a mic and a button
* AIRI repo preserved for later if needed

## Live URL

[https://brainwave-ten.vercel.app](<https://brainwave-ten.vercel.app>) (deployed as Brainwave)

## Repo

chillkimtest-oss/hey-chill

---

## Features (cumulative — updated after each milestone)

### Push-to-Talk

* Press and hold button to record voice
* Uses Web Speech API for real-time transcription
* Visual feedback during recording

### Voice Activity Detection (VAD)

* Auto-stops recording after 3.5 seconds of silence
* Allows natural pauses between sentences without cutting off

### Transcription

* Real-time speech-to-text via Web Speech API
* Interim results shown during recording
* Final transcript captured on recording end

### Slack Integration

* Sends transcription to Chill agent via n8n webhook
* Webhook URL: /webhook/hey-chill
* Message delivered to Slack channel

### PWA

* Installable as progressive web app
* Service worker for offline shell
* Works on mobile and desktop browsers","url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/project/hey-chill-335516e3da6b","createdAt":"2026-03-01T02:51:01.776Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-07T23:21:25.560Z","startDate":"2026-03-01","targetDate":null,"labels":[],"initiatives":[],"lead":{},"status":{"id":"45fae361-8f51-4684-a479-68682e997bd2","name":"In Progress"},"teams":[{"id":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd","name":"Forge","key":"FRG"}],"resources":[{"type":"link","id":"54361443-e0ef-4000-a58c-1802bff05f45","label":"Brainwave (LAN)","url":"https://192.168.101.151:3005","createdAt":"2026-03-07T00:24:07.760Z"},{"type":"link","id":"92fbc355-5dce-4fca-b9ee-d21de9658b19","label":"GitHub","url":"https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/hey-chill","createdAt":"2026-03-07T00:24:07.564Z"},{"type":"link","id":"c515dc96-8458-4df6-b82e-ed59939657a3","label":"Hey Chill PWA","url":"https://hey-chill.vercel.app","createdAt":"2026-03-07T00:24:07.376Z"}]}

  "function": "() => {\n  return {\n    title: document.title,\n    manifest: document.querySelector('link[rel=\"manifest\"]')?.href,\n    sw: 'serviceWorker' in navigator ? }

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #36
mcp__chrome_devtools__evaluate_script TOOL CALL 
{
  "function": "async () => {\n  // Check service worker registration\n  const swRegs = await navigator.serviceWorker.getRegistrations();\n  const swInfo = swRegs.map(r => ({ scope: r.scope, state: r.active?.state, scriptURL: r.active?.scriptURL }));\n  \n  // Fetch manifest\n  const manifestResp = await fetch('/manifest.json');\n  const manifest = await manifestResp.json();\n  \n  return { swRegistrations: swInfo, manifest };\n}"
}
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_019o4nimrj2N8RRshgsAuKU3",
    "name": "mcp__chrome_devtools__evaluate_script",
    "input": {
      "function": "async () => {\n  // Check service worker registration\n  const swRegs = await navigator.serviceWorker.getRegistrations();\n  const swInfo = swRegs.map(r => ({ scope: r.scope, state: r.active?.state, scriptURL: r.active?.scriptURL }));\n  \n  // Fetch manifest\n  const manifestResp = await fetch('/manifest.json');\n  const manifest = await manifestResp.json();\n  \n  return { swRegistrations: swInfo, manifest };\n}"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

```json
"<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang=\"en\">
<head>
  <meta charset=\"UTF-8\" />
  <meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, viewport-fit=cover\" />
  <meta name=\"theme-color\" content=\"#1a1a2e\" />
  <meta name=\"description\" content=\"Voice-first AI agent — Hey Chill\" />
  <meta name=\"mobile-web-app-capable\" content=\"yes\" />
  <meta name=\"apple-mobile-web-app-capable\" content=\"yes\" />
  <meta name=\"apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style\" content=\"black-translucent\" />
  <meta name=\"apple-mobile-web-app-title\" content=\"Chill\" />
  <link rel=\"manifest\" href=\"manifest.json\" />
  <link rel=\"icon\" href=\"icons/icon-192.svg\" type=\"image/svg+xml\" />
  <link rel=\"apple-touch-icon\" href=\"icons/icon-192.svg\" />
  <link rel=\"stylesheet\" href=\"style.css\" />
  <title>Hey Chill</title>
</head>
<body>
  <div id=\"app\">
    <!-- Header -->
    <header class=\"header\">
      <div class=\"header-title\">
        <span class=\"logo-dot\"></span>
        <h1>Chill</h1>
      </div>
      <button class=\"icon-btn\" id=\"settings-btn\" aria-label=\"Settings\" title=\"Settings\">
        <svg viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\">
          <circle cx=\"12\" cy=\"12\" r=\"3\"/>
          <path d=\"M19.4 15a1.65 1.65 0 0 0 .33 1.82l.06.06a2 2 0 0 1-2.83 2.83l-.06-.06a1.65 1.65 0 0 0-1.82-.33 1.65 1.65 0 0 0-1 1.51V21a2 2 0 0 1-4 0v-.09A1.65 1.65 0 0 0 9 19.4a1.65 1.65 0 0 0-1.82.33l-.06.06a2 2 0 0 1-2.83-2.83l.06-.06A1.65 1.65 0 0 0 4.68 15a1.65 1.65 0 0 0-1.51-1H3a2 2 0 0 1 0-4h.09A1.65 1.65 0 0 0 4.6 9a1.65 1.65 0 0 0-.33-1.82l-.06-.06a2 2 0 0 1 2.83-2.83l.06.06A1.65 1.65 0 0 0 9 4.68a1.65 1.65 0 0 0 1-1.51V3a2 2 0 0 1 4 0v.09a1.65 1.65 0 0 0 1 1.51 1.65 1.65 0 0 0 1.82-.33l.06-.06a2 2 0 0 1 2.83 2.83l-.06.06A1.65 1.65 0 0 0 19.4 9a1.65 1.65 0 0 0 1.51 1H21a2 2 0 0 1 0 4h-.09a1.65 1.65 0 0 0-1.51 1z\"/>
        </svg>
      </button>
    </header>

    <!-- Message history -->
    <main class=\"messages\" id=\"messages\" aria-live=\"polite\" aria-label=\"Message history\">
      <div class=\"messages-empty\" id=\"messages-empty\">
        <p>Your messages will appear here</p>
      </div>
    </main>

    <!-- Transcript preview (shown after speaking) -->
    <div class=\"transcript-bar\" id=\"transcript-bar\" hidden>
      <p class=\"transcript-text\" id=\"transcript-text\"></p>
      <div class=\"transcript-actions\">
        <button class=\"btn-ghost\" id=\"discard-btn\">Discard</button>
        <button class=\"btn-send\" id=\"send-btn\">Send</button>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Live transcript (shown while listening) -->
    <div class=\"live-transcript\" id=\"live-transcript\" hidden>
      <p id=\"live-text\">Listening…</p>
    </div>

    <!-- Mic area -->
    <section class=\"mic-section\">
      <div class=\"mic-hint\" id=\"mic-hint\">Tap to speak</div>
      <div class=\"mic-ring\" id=\"mic-ring\">
        <button
          class=\"mic-btn\"
          id=\"mic-btn\"
          aria-label=\"Start recording\"
          aria-pressed=\"false\"
        >
          <svg class=\"mic-icon\" viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\" fill=\"currentColor\">
            <path d=\"M12 1a4 4 0 0 1 4 4v7a4 4 0 0 1-8 0V5a4 4 0 0 1 4-4z\"/>
            <path d=\"M19 11a1 1 0 0 1 1 1 8 8 0 0 1-16 0 1 1 0 1 1 2 0 6 6 0 0 0 12 0 1 1 0 0 1 1-1z\"/>
            <line x1=\"12\" y1=\"19\" x2=\"12\" y2=\"23\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\"/>
            <line x1=\"8\" y1=\"23\" x2=\"16\" y2=\"23\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\"/>
          </svg>
        </button>
      </div>
    </section>

    <!-- Not supported banner -->
    <div class=\"not-supported\" id=\"not-supported\" hidden>
      <p>Speech recognition is not supported in this browser. Try Chrome or Edge.</p>
    </div>
  </div>

  <!-- Settings modal -->
  <div class=\"modal-overlay\" id=\"modal-overlay\" hidden>
    <div class=\"modal\" role=\"dialog\" aria-modal=\"true\" aria-labelledby=\"modal-title\">
      <div class=\"modal-header\">
        <h2 id=\"modal-title\">Settings</h2>
        <button class=\"icon-btn\" id=\"modal-close\" aria-label=\"Close settings\">
          <svg viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\">
            <line x1=\"18\" y1=\"6\" x2=\"6\" y2=\"18\"/>
            <line x1=\"6\" y1=\"6\" x2=\"18\" y2=\"18\"/>
          </svg>
        </button>
      </div>
      <div class=\"modal-body\">
        <label for=\"webhook-url\">Webhook URL</label>
        <input
          type=\"url\"
          id=\"webhook-url\"
          placeholder=\"https://your-webhook.example.com/hook\"
          autocomplete=\"off\"
          spellcheck=\"false\"
        />
        <p class=\"field-hint\">Messages will be POSTed here as JSON.</p>
      </div>
      <div class=\"modal-footer\">
        <button class=\"btn-send\" id=\"save-settings\">Save</button>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <script src=\"app.js\"></script>
</body>
</html>
"
```

USERMESSAGE #41
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
# evaluate_script response
Script ran on page and returned:
```json
"'use strict';

/* ===== DOM refs ===== */
const micBtn        = document.getElementById('mic-btn');
const micRing       = document.getElementById('mic-ring');
const micHint       = document.getElementById('mic-hint');
const liveTranscript = document.getElementById('live-transcript');
const liveText      = document.getElementById('live-text');
const transcriptBar = document.getElementById('transcript-bar');
const transcriptText = document.getElementById('transcript-text');
const sendBtn       = document.getElementById('send-btn');
const discardBtn    = document.getElementById('discard-btn');
const messagesEl    = document.getElementById('messages');
const messagesEmpty = document.getElementById('messages-empty');
const notSupported  = document.getElementById('not-supported');
const settingsBtn   = document.getElementById('settings-btn');
const modalOverlay  = document.getElementById('modal-overlay');
const modalClose    = document.getElementById('modal-close');
const webhookInput  = document.getElementById('webhook-url');
const saveSettings  = document.getElementById('save-settings');

/* ===== State ===== */
let recognition     = null;
let isListening     = false;
let finalTranscript = '';
let interimText     = '';
let silenceTimer    = null;
const SILENCE_DELAY = 3500; // ms of silence before auto-stop

/* ===== Service Worker ===== */
if ('serviceWorker' in navigator) {
  navigator.serviceWorker.register('sw.js').catch(() => {/* silent */});
}

/* ===== Speech Recognition setup ===== */
const SpeechRecognition = window.SpeechRecognition || window.webkitSpeechRecognition;

if (!SpeechRecognition) {
  notSupported.hidden = false;
  micBtn.disabled = true;
  micBtn.setAttribute('aria-disabled', 'true');
} else {
  initRecognition();
}

function initRecognition() {
  recognition = new SpeechRecognition();
  recognition.continuous     = true;
  recognition.interimResults = true;
  recognition.lang           = navigator.language || 'en-US';
  recognition.maxAlternatives = 1;

  recognition.onstart = () => {
    isListening = true;
    finalTranscript = '';
    interimText = '';
    setRecordingUI(true);
  };

  recognition.onresult = (event) => {
    let interim = '';
    for (let i = event.resultIndex; i < event.results.length; i++) {
      const result = event.results[i];
      if (result.isFinal) {
        finalTranscript += result[0].transcript + ' ';
      } else {
        interim += result[0].transcript;
      }
    }
    interimText = interim;
    const displayText = (finalTranscript + interim).trim();
    updateLiveText(displayText);

    // Reset silence timer on every result
    resetSilenceTimer();
  };

  recognition.onerror = (event) => {
    // 'no-speech' is common and expected — just stop cleanly
    if (event.error !== 'no-speech') {
      console.warn('Speech recognition error:', event.error);
    }
    stopListening();
  };

  recognition.onend = () => {
    // onend fires when recognition stops for any reason
    if (isListening) {
      // We didn't call stop manually yet; auto-stop happened
      finishRecording();
    }
  };
}

function resetSilenceTimer() {
  clearTimeout(silenceTimer);
  silenceTimer = setTimeout(() => {
    if (isListening) stopListening();
  }, SILENCE_DELAY);
}

/* ===== Recording controls ===== */
function startListening() {
  if (!recognition || isListening) return;
  hideTranscriptBar();

  try {
    recognition.start();
  } catch (e) {
    console.warn('Could not start recognition:', e);
    // Re-init in case of invalid state
    initRecognition();
    try { recognition.start(); } catch (_) {}
  }

  resetSilenceTimer();
}

function stopListening() {
  clearTimeout(silenceTimer);
  isListening = false; // mark before calling stop so onend doesn't re-trigger finishRecording
  if (recognition) {
    try { recognition.stop(); } catch (_) {}
  }
  finishRecording();
}

function finishRecording() {
  setRecordingUI(false);
  const text = finalTranscript.trim();
  if (text) {
    showTranscriptBar(text);
  }
}

/* ===== UI state helpers ===== */
function setRecordingUI(recording) {
  micBtn.classList.toggle('recording', recording);
  micRing.classList.toggle('recording', recording);
  micBtn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', recording ? 'true' : 'false');
  micBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', recording ? 'Stop recording' : 'Start recording');
  micHint.textContent = recording ? 'Tap to stop' : 'Tap to speak';
  micHint.classList.toggle('active', recording);
  liveTranscript.hidden = !recording;
  if (!recording) {
    liveText.textContent = 'Listening…';
    liveText.classList.remove('has-text');
  }
}

function updateLiveText(text) {
  if (text) {
    liveText.textContent = text;
    liveText.classList.add('has-text');
  } else {
    liveText.textContent = 'Listening…';
    liveText.classList.remove('has-text');
  }
}

function showTranscriptBar(text) {
  transcriptText.textContent = text;
  transcriptBar.hidden = false;
}

function hideTranscriptBar() {
  transcriptBar.hidden = true;
  transcriptText.textContent = '';
}

/* ===== Mic button click (toggle) ===== */
micBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
  if (isListening) {
    stopListening();
  } else {
    startListening();
  }
});

/* ===== Send / Discard ===== */
sendBtn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
  const text = transcriptText.textContent.trim();
  if (!text) return;
  hideTranscriptBar();
  await sendMessage(text);
});

discardBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
  hideTranscriptBar();
});

/* ===== Webhook send ===== */
async function sendMessage(text) {
  const webhookUrl = localStorage.getItem('webhookUrl') || '';
  const timestamp  = new Date().toISOString();
  const payload    = { text, source: 'hey-chill', timestamp };

  // Add message to history immediately
  const msgEl = addMessage(text, timestamp, 'sending');

  if (!webhookUrl) {
    updateMessageStatus(msgEl, 'no-webhook');
    return;
  }

  try {
    const res = await fetch(webhookUrl, {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify(payload),
    });
    if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${res.status}`);
    updateMessageStatus(msgEl, 'sent');
  } catch (err) {
    console.warn('Webhook error:', err);
    updateMessageStatus(msgEl, 'error');
  }
}

/* ===== Message history ===== */
function addMessage(text, timestamp, status) {
  // Hide empty state
  messagesEmpty.style.display = 'none';

  const el = document.createElement('div');
  el.className = `message ${status}`;

  const timeStr = new Date(timestamp).toLocaleTimeString([], {
    hour: '2-digit',
    minute: '2-digit',
  });

  const statusLabel = {
    sending:    'Sending…',
    sent:       'Sent',
    error:      'Failed',
    'no-webhook': 'No webhook',
  };

  el.innerHTML = `
    <p class=\"message-text\">${escapeHtml(text)}</p>
    <div class=\"message-meta\">
      <span class=\"message-time\">${timeStr}</span>
      <span class=\"message-status\">${statusLabel[status] || status}</span>
    </div>
  `;

  messagesEl.appendChild(el);
  el.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'end' });
  return el;
}

function updateMessageStatus(el, status) {
  el.className = `message ${status}`;
  const statusEl = el.querySelector('.message-status');
  const labels = {
    sent:         'Sent',
    error:        'Failed',
    'no-webhook': 'No webhook set',
  };
  if (statusEl) statusEl.textContent = labels[status] || status;
}

function escapeHtml(str) {
  return str
    .replace(/&/g, '&amp;')
    .replace(/</g, '&lt;')
    .replace(/>/g, '&gt;')
    .replace(/\"/g, '&quot;')
    .replace(/'/g, '&#39;');
}

/* ===== Settings modal ===== */
settingsBtn.addEventListener('click', openSettings);
modalClose.addEventListener('click',  closeSettings);
modalOverlay.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
  if (e.target === modalOverlay) closeSettings();
});

document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {
  if (e.key === 'Escape') closeSettings();
});

saveSettings.addEventListener('click', () => {
  const url = webhookInput.value.trim();
  localStorage.setItem('webhookUrl', url);
  closeSettings();
});

function openSettings() {
  webhookInput.value = localStorage.getItem('webhookUrl') || '';
  modalOverlay.hidden = false;
  webhookInput.focus();
}

function closeSettings() {
  modalOverlay.hidden = true;
}
"
```
▶ VIEW RAW DATA

  "content": {
    "tool_use_id": "toolu_015Zjr1Uwi365DTjAyXNDnYe",
    "type": "tool_result",
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "text",
        "text": "# evaluate_script response\nScript ran on page and returned:\n```json\n\"'use strict';\\n\\n/* ===== DOM refs ===== */\\nconst micBtn        = document.getElementById('mic-btn');\\nconst micRing       = document.getElementById('mic-ring');\\nconst micHint       = document.getElementById('mic-hint');\\nconst liveTranscript = document.getElementById('live-transcript');\\nconst liveText      = document.getElementById('live-text');\\nconst transcriptBar = document.getElementById('transcript-bar');\\nconst transcriptText = document.getElementById('transcript-text');\\nconst sendBtn       = document.getElementById('send-btn');\\nconst discardBtn    = document.getElementById('discard-btn');\\nconst messagesEl    = document.getElementById('messages');\\nconst messagesEmpty = document.getElementById('messages-empty');\\nconst notSupported  = document.getElementById('not-supported');\\nconst settingsBtn   = document.getElementById('settings-btn');\\nconst modalOverlay  = document.getElementById('modal-overlay');\\nconst modalClose    = document.getElementById('modal-close');\\nconst webhookInput  = document.getElementById('webhook-url');\\nconst saveSettings  = document.getElementById('save-settings');\\n\\n/* ===== State ===== */\\nlet recognition     = null;\\nlet isListening     = false;\\nlet finalTranscript = '';\\nlet interimText     = '';\\nlet silenceTimer    = null;\\nconst SILENCE_DELAY = 3500; // ms of silence before auto-stop\\n\\n/* ===== Service Worker ===== */\\nif ('serviceWorker' in navigator) {\\n  navigator.serviceWorker.register('sw.js').catch(() => {/* silent */});\\n}\\n\\n/* ===== Speech Recognition setup ===== */\\nconst SpeechRecognition = window.SpeechRecognition || window.webkitSpeechRecognition;\\n\\nif (!SpeechRecognition) {\\n  notSupported.hidden = false;\\n  micBtn.disabled = true;\\n  micBtn.setAttribute('aria-disabled', 'true');\\n} else {\\n  initRecognition();\\n}\\n\\nfunction initRecognition() {\\n  recognition = new SpeechRecognition();\\n  recognition.continuous     = true;\\n  recognition.interimResults = true;\\n  recognition.lang           = navigator.language || 'en-US';\\n  recognition.maxAlternatives = 1;\\n\\n  recognition.onstart = () => {\\n    isListening = true;\\n    finalTranscript = '';\\n    interimText = '';\\n    setRecordingUI(true);\\n  };\\n\\n  recognition.onresult = (event) => {\\n    let interim = '';\\n    for (let i = event.resultIndex; i < event.results.length; i++) {\\n      const result = event.results[i];\\n      if (result.isFinal) {\\n        finalTranscript += result[0].transcript + ' ';\\n      } else {\\n        interim += result[0].transcript;\\n      }\\n    }\\n    interimText = interim;\\n    const displayText = (finalTranscript + interim).trim();\\n    updateLiveText(displayText);\\n\\n    // Reset silence timer on every result\\n    resetSilenceTimer();\\n  };\\n\\n  recognition.onerror = (event) => {\\n    // 'no-speech' is common and expected \u2014 just stop cleanly\\n    if (event.error !== 'no-speech') {\\n      console.warn('Speech recognition error:', event.error);\\n    }\\n    stopListening();\\n  };\\n\\n  recognition.onend = () => {\\n    // onend fires when recognition stops for any reason\\n    if (isListening) {\\n      // We didn't call stop manually yet; auto-stop happened\\n      finishRecording();\\n    }\\n  };\\n}\\n\\nfunction resetSilenceTimer() {\\n  clearTimeout(silenceTimer);\\n  silenceTimer = setTimeout(() => {\\n    if (isListening) stopListening();\\n  }, SILENCE_DELAY);\\n}\\n\\n/* ===== Recording controls ===== */\\nfunction startListening() {\\n  if (!recognition || isListening) return;\\n  hideTranscriptBar();\\n\\n  try {\\n    recognition.start();\\n  } catch (e) {\\n    console.warn('Could not start recognition:', e);\\n    // Re-init in case of invalid state\\n    initRecognition();\\n    try { recognition.start(); } catch (_) {}\\n  }\\n\\n  resetSilenceTimer();\\n}\\n\\nfunction stopListening() {\\n  clearTimeout(silenceTimer);\\n  isListening = false; // mark before calling stop so onend doesn't re-trigger finishRecording\\n  if (recognition) {\\n    try { recognition.stop(); } catch (_) {}\\n  }\\n  finishRecording();\\n}\\n\\nfunction finishRecording() {\\n  setRecordingUI(false);\\n  const text = finalTranscript.trim();\\n  if (text) {\\n    showTranscriptBar(text);\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/* ===== UI state helpers ===== */\\nfunction setRecordingUI(recording) {\\n  micBtn.classList.toggle('recording', recording);\\n  micRing.classList.toggle('recording', recording);\\n  micBtn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', recording ? 'true' : 'false');\\n  micBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', recording ? 'Stop recording' : 'Start recording');\\n  micHint.textContent = recording ? 'Tap to stop' : 'Tap to speak';\\n  micHint.classList.toggle('active', recording);\\n  liveTranscript.hidden = !recording;\\n  if (!recording) {\\n    liveText.textContent = 'Listening\u2026';\\n    liveText.classList.remove('has-text');\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nfunction updateLiveText(text) {\\n  if (text) {\\n    liveText.textContent = text;\\n    liveText.classList.add('has-text');\\n  } else {\\n    liveText.textContent = 'Listening\u2026';\\n    liveText.classList.remove('has-text');\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nfunction showTranscriptBar(text) {\\n  transcriptText.textContent = text;\\n  transcriptBar.hidden = false;\\n}\\n\\nfunction hideTranscriptBar() {\\n  transcriptBar.hidden = true;\\n  transcriptText.textContent = '';\\n}\\n\\n/* ===== Mic button click (toggle) ===== */\\nmicBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {\\n  if (isListening) {\\n    stopListening();\\n  } else {\\n    startListening();\\n  }\\n});\\n\\n/* ===== Send / Discard ===== */\\nsendBtn.addEventListener('click', async () => {\\n  const text = transcriptText.textContent.trim();\\n  if (!text) return;\\n  hideTranscriptBar();\\n  await sendMessage(text);\\n});\\n\\ndiscardBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {\\n  hideTranscriptBar();\\n});\\n\\n/* ===== Webhook send ===== */\\nasync function sendMessage(text) {\\n  const webhookUrl = localStorage.getItem('webhookUrl') || '';\\n  const timestamp  = new Date().toISOString();\\n  const payload    = { text, source: 'hey-chill', timestamp };\\n\\n  // Add message to history immediately\\n  const msgEl = addMessage(text, timestamp, 'sending');\\n\\n  if (!webhookUrl) {\\n    updateMessageStatus(msgEl, 'no-webhook');\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  try {\\n    const res = await fetch(webhookUrl, {\\n      method: 'POST',\\n      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\\n      body: JSON.stringify(payload),\\n    });\\n    if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${res.status}`);\\n    updateMessageStatus(msgEl, 'sent');\\n  } catch (err) {\\n    console.warn('Webhook error:', err);\\n    updateMessageStatus(msgEl, 'error');\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/* ===== Message history ===== */\\nfunction addMessage(text, timestamp, status) {\\n  // Hide empty state\\n  messagesEmpty.style.display = 'none';\\n\\n  const el = document.createElement('div');\\n  el.className = `message ${status}`;\\n\\n  const timeStr = new Date(timestamp).toLocaleTimeString([], {\\n    hour: '2-digit',\\n    minute: '2-digit',\\n  });\\n\\n  const statusLabel = {\\n    sending:    'Sending\u2026',\\n    sent:       'Sent',\\n    error:      'Failed',\\n    'no-webhook': 'No webhook',\\n  };\\n\\n  el.innerHTML = `\\n    <p class=\\\"message-text\\\">${escapeHtml(text)}</p>\\n    <div class=\\\"message-meta\\\">\\n      <span class=\\\"message-time\\\">${timeStr}</span>\\n      <span class=\\\"message-status\\\">${statusLabel[status] || status}</span>\\n    </div>\\n  `;\\n\\n  messagesEl.appendChild(el);\\n  el.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'end' });\\n  return el;\\n}\\n\\nfunction updateMessageStatus(el, status) {\\n  el.className = `message ${status}`;\\n  const statusEl = el.querySelector('.message-status');\\n  const labels = {\\n    sent:         'Sent',\\n    error:        'Failed',\\n    'no-webhook': 'No webhook set',\\n  };\\n  if (statusEl) statusEl.textContent = labels[status] || status;\\n}\\n\\nfunction escapeHtml(str) {\\n  return str\\n    .replace(/&/g, '&amp;')\\n    .replace(/</g, '&lt;')\\n    .replace(/>/g, '&gt;')\\n    .replace(/\\\"/g, '&quot;')\\n    .replace(/'/g, '&#39;');\\n}\\n\\n/* ===== Settings modal ===== */\\nsettingsBtn.addEventListener('click', openSettings);\\nmodalClose.addEventListener('click',  closeSettings);\\nmodalOverlay.addEventListener('click', (e) => {\\n  if (e.target === modalOverlay) closeSettings();\\n});\\n\\ndocument.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {\\n  if (e.key === 'Escape') closeSettings();\\n});\\n\\nsaveSettings.addEventListener('click', () => {\\n  const url = webhookInput.value.trim();\\n  localStorage.setItem('webhookUrl', url);\\n  closeSettings();\\n});\\n\\nfunction openSettings() {\\n  webhookInput.value = localStorage.getItem('webhookUrl') || '';\\n  modalOverlay.hidden = false;\\n  webhookInput.focus();\\n}\\n\\nfunction closeSettings() {\\n  modalOverlay.hidden = true;\\n}\\n\"\n```"
      }
    ]
  }
}
ASSISTANTMESSAGE #42
Now I have a thorough understanding of the Hey Chill app. Let me plan and write the test suite. Key features to test: 1. **Push-to-Talk UI**: mic button, visual feedback, aria attributes, mic-ring animation 2. **VAD silence timeout (3.5s)**: SILENCE_DELAY = 3500ms; need to mock SpeechRecognition and timer 3. **Transcription display**: live text updates, transcript bar appears after recording 4. **Slack webhook send**: network interception to verify fetch call with correct payload 5. **PWA installability**: manifest with correct fields, service worker registered 6. **Service worker**: registered, activated Now I'm setting up Playwright with the test structure organized into smoke tests for critical paths and regression tests for full coverage, using the Vercel deployment URL as the base. For the regression suite, I'm planning to test the core features: push-to-talk button state changes, live transcription with mocked speech recognition, voice activity detection timing, webhook payload verification through network interception, settings persistence to localStorage, message history display, PWA capabilities including manifest and service worker, and the fallback banner when speech recognition isn't available. I'll start by initializing the Node project, installing Playwright, configuring it, and writing out the test files. Now I'm setting up the SpeechRecognition mock using page.addInitScript to inject it before the page loads, then I'll write the actual test cases.

{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/playwright.config.ts",
  "content": "import { defineConfig, devices } from '@playwright/test';\nimport * as dotenv from 'dotenv';\n\ndotenv.config();\n\nconst BASE_URL = process.env.BASE_URL || 'https://hey-chill.vercel.app';\n\nexport default defineConfig({\n  testDir: './tests',\n  testMatch: '**/*.spec.ts',\n  fullyParallel: true,\n  forbidOnly: !!process.env.CI,\n  retries: process.env.CI ? 2 : 0,\n  workers: process.env.CI ? 1 : undefined,\n  reporter: [['html'], ['list']],\n  use: {\n    baseURL: BASE_URL,\n    trace: 'on-first-retry',\n    screenshot: 'only-on-failure',\n  },\n  projects: [\n    {\n      name: 'chromium',\n      use: {\n        ...devices['Desktop Chrome'],\n        // Grant mic permission so the app doesn't block on permission prompts\n        permissions: ['microphone'],\n      },\n    },\n  ],\n});\n"
}

      "content": "import { defineConfig, devices } from '@playwright/test';\nimport * as dotenv from 'dotenv';\n\ndotenv.config();\n\nconst BASE_URL = process.env.BASE_URL || 'https://hey-chill.vercel.app';\n\nexport default defineConfig({\n  testDir: './tests',\n  testMatch: '**/*.spec.ts',\n  fullyParallel: true,\n  forbidOnly: !!process.env.CI,\n  retries: process.env.CI ? 2 : 0,\n  workers: process.env.CI ? 1 : undefined,\n  reporter: [['html'], ['list']],\n  use: {\n    baseURL: BASE_URL,\n    trace: 'on-first-retry',\n    screenshot: 'only-on-failure',\n  },\n  projects: [\n    {\n      name: 'chromium',\n      use: {\n        ...devices['Desktop Chrome'],\n        // Grant mic permission so the app doesn't block on permission prompts\n        permissions: ['microphone'],\n      },\n    },\n  ],\n});\n"

Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/helpers/speech-mock.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * SpeechRecognition mock helpers.\n *\n * Web Speech API is unavailable in headless Chromium, so we inject a\n * controllable mock before page scripts execute.  Tests call the exposed\n * helpers via page.evaluate() to drive the mock.\n */\n\n/**\n * Script injected via page.addInitScript().\n * Replaces window.SpeechRecognition / webkitSpeechRecognition with a\n * controllable stub and exposes a __speechMock controller object.\n */\nexport const SPEECH_MOCK_SCRIPT = `\n(function () {\n  'use strict';\n\n  // Shared state accessible from test helpers\n  window.__speechMock = {\n    instances: [],\n    lastInstance: null,\n  };\n\n  class MockSpeechRecognition extends EventTarget {\n    constructor() {\n      super();\n      this.continuous      = false;\n      this.interimResults  = false;\n      this.lang            = 'en-US';\n      this.maxAlternatives = 1;\n      this._started        = false;\n      window.__speechMock.instances.push(this);\n      window.__speechMock.lastInstance = this;\n    }\n\n    start() {\n      if (this._started) throw new DOMException('Already started', 'InvalidStateError');\n      this._started = true;\n      // Fire 'start' async to match real API\n      Promise.resolve().then(() => this._fire('start'));\n    }\n\n    stop() {\n      if (!this._started) return;\n      this._started = false;\n      Promise.resolve().then(() => {\n        this._fire('end');\n      });\n    }\n\n    abort() { this.stop(); }\n\n    /** Helper: synthesise a speech result event */\n    _emitResult(transcript, isFinal) {\n      const result = {\n        isFinal,\n        0: { transcript, confidence: 0.9 },\n        length: 1,\n      };\n      const results = { 0: result, length: 1, resultIndex: 0 };\n      // Build a SpeechRecognitionResultList-like object\n      const event = new Event('result');\n      event.resultIndex = 0;\n      event.results = {\n        length: 1,\n        item(i) { return this[i]; },\n        0: result,\n      };\n      this.dispatchEvent(event);\n    }\n\n    /** Helper: fire a named event (onXxx + dispatchEvent) */\n    _fire(name, detail) {\n      const ev = detail || new Event(name);\n      if (!detail) ev.type = name; // ensure correct type\n      const handler = this['on' + name];\n      if (typeof handler === 'function') handler.call(this, ev);\n      this.dispatchEvent(ev);\n    }\n  }\n\n  window.SpeechRecognition = MockSpeechRecognition;\n  window.webkitSpeechRecognition = MockSpeechRecognition;\n})();\n`;\n\n/**\n * Emit an interim speech result via the mock.\n */\nexport async function emitInterimResult(page: import('@playwright/test').Page, transcript: string) {\n  await page.evaluate((t) => {\n    const inst = window.__speechMock?.lastInstance;\n    if (!inst) throw new Error('No SpeechRecognition instance found');\n    inst._emitResult(t, false);\n  }, transcript);\n}\n\n/**\n * Emit a final speech result via the mock.\n */\nexport async function emitFinalResult(page: import('@playwright/test').Page, transcript: string) {\n  await page.evaluate((t) => {\n    const inst = window.__speechMock?.lastInstance;\n    if (!inst) throw new Error('No SpeechRecognition instance found');\n    inst._emitResult(t, true);\n  }, transcript);\n}\n\n/**\n * Trigger recognition end (simulates silence / auto-stop).\n */\nexport async function endRecognition(page: import('@playwright/test').Page) {\n  await page.evaluate(() => {\n    const inst = window.__speechMock?.lastInstance;\n    if (!inst) throw new Error('No SpeechRecognition instance found');\n    inst._started = false;\n    const ev = new Event('end');\n    const handler = inst['onend'];\n    if (typeof handler === 'function') handler.call(inst, ev);\n    inst.dispatchEvent(ev);\n  });\n}\n"
}

    "name": "Write",
    "input": {
      "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/helpers/speech-mock.ts",
      "content": "/**\n * SpeechRecognition mock helpers.\n *\n * Web Speech API is unavailable in headless Chromium, so we inject a\n * controllable mock before page scripts execute.  Tests call the exposed\n * helpers via page.evaluate() to drive the mock.\n */\n\n/**\n * Script injected via page.addInitScript().\n * Replaces window.SpeechRecognition / webkitSpeechRecognition with a\n * controllable stub and exposes a __speechMock controller object.\n */\nexport const SPEECH_MOCK_SCRIPT = `\n(function () {\n  'use strict';\n\n  // Shared state accessible from test helpers\n  window.__speechMock = {\n    instances: [],\n    lastInstance: null,\n  };\n\n  class MockSpeechRecognition extends EventTarget {\n    constructor() {\n      super();\n      this.continuous      = false;\n      this.interimResults  = false;\n      this.lang            = 'en-US';\n      this.maxAlternatives = 1;\n      this._started        = false;\n      window.__speechMock.instances.push(this);\n      window.__speechMock.lastInstance = this;\n    }\n\n    start() {\n      if (this._started) throw new DOMException('Already started', 'InvalidStateError');\n      this._started = true;\n      // Fire 'start' async to match real API\n      Promise.resolve().then(() => this._fire('start'));\n    }\n\n    stop() {\n      if (!this._started) return;\n      this._started = false;\n      Promise.resolve().then(() => {\n        this._fire('end');\n      });\n    }\n\n    abort() { this.stop(); }\n\n    /** Helper: synthesise a speech result event */\n    _emitResult(transcript, isFinal) {\n      const result = {\n        isFinal,\n        0: { transcript, confidence: 0.9 },\n        length: 1,\n      };\n      const results = { 0: result, length: 1, resultIndex: 0 };\n      // Build a SpeechRecognitionResultList-like object\n      const event = new Event('result');\n      event.resultIndex = 0;\n      event.results = {\n        length: 1,\n        item(i) { return this[i]; },\n        0: result,\n      };\n      this.dispatchEvent(event);\n    }\n\n    /** Helper: fire a named event (onXxx + dispatchEvent) */\n    _fire(name, detail) {\n      const ev = detail || new Event(name);\n      if (!detail) ev.type = name; // ensure correct type\n      const handler = this['on' + name];\n      if (typeof handler === 'function') handler.call(this, ev);\n      this.dispatchEvent(ev);\n    }\n  }\n\n  window.SpeechRecognition = MockSpeechRecognition;\n  window.webkitSpeechRecognition = MockSpeechRecognition;\n})();\n`;\n\n/**\n * Emit an interim speech result via the mock.\n */\nexport async function emitInterimResult(page: import('@playwright/test').Page, transcript: string) {\n  await page.evaluate((t) => {\n    const inst = window.__speechMock?.lastInstance;\n    if (!inst) throw new Error('No SpeechRecognition instance found');\n    inst._emitResult(t, false);\n  }, transcript);\n}\n\n/**\n * Emit a final speech result via the mock.\n */\nexport async function emitFinalResult(page: import('@playwright/test').Page, transcript: string) {\n  await page.evaluate((t) => {\n    const inst = window.__speechMock?.lastInstance;\n    if (!inst) throw new Error('No SpeechRecognition instance found');\n    inst._emitResult(t, true);\n  }, transcript);\n}\n\n/**\n * Trigger recognition end (simulates silence / auto-stop).\n */\nexport async function endRecognition(page: import('@playwright/test').Page) {\n  await page.evaluate(() => {\n    const inst = window.__speechMock?.lastInstance;\n    if (!inst) throw new Error('No SpeechRecognition instance found');\n    inst._started = false;\n    const ev = new Event('end');\n    const handler = inst['onend'];\n    if (typeof handler === 'function') handler.call(inst, ev);\n    inst.dispatchEvent(ev);\n  });\n}\n"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/smoke/smoke.spec.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * @smoke\n *\n * Smoke tests \u2014 fast sanity checks that the deployed app is reachable and\n * the critical shell elements are present.  These must pass before regression\n * tests are meaningful.\n */\n\nimport { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';\nimport { SPEECH_MOCK_SCRIPT } from '../helpers/speech-mock';\n\ntest.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.addInitScript(SPEECH_MOCK_SCRIPT);\n});\n\ntest('@smoke page loads with correct title', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.goto('/');\n  await expect(page).toHaveTitle('Hey Chill');\n});\n\ntest('@smoke app heading is \"Chill\"', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.goto('/');\n  await expect(page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Chill' })).toBeVisible();\n});\n\ntest('@smoke mic button is present and enabled', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.goto('/');\n  const micBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Start recording' });\n  await expect(micBtn).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(micBtn).toBeEnabled();\n});\n\ntest('@smoke settings button opens modal', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.goto('/');\n  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Settings' }).click();\n  await expect(page.getByRole('dialog')).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByText('Settings')).toBeVisible();\n});\n\ntest('@smoke manifest.json is reachable and valid', async ({ page }) => {\n  const resp = await page.request.get('/manifest.json');\n  expect(resp.ok()).toBe(true);\n  const manifest = await resp.json();\n  expect(manifest.name).toBe('Hey Chill');\n  expect(manifest.display).toBe('standalone');\n  expect(Array.isArray(manifest.icons)).toBe(true);\n  expect(manifest.icons.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n});\n\ntest('@smoke service worker is registered and active', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.goto('/');\n  // Allow SW to register\n  await page.waitForTimeout(1500);\n  const swState = await page.evaluate(async () => {\n    const regs = await navigator.serviceWorker.getRegistrations();\n    return regs.map((r) => ({\n      scope: r.scope,\n      state: r.active?.state ?? null,\n    }));\n  });\n  expect(swState.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n  expect(swState[0].state).toBe('activated');\n});\n\ntest('@smoke sw.js is reachable', async ({ page }) => {\n  const resp = await page.request.get('/sw.js');\n  expect(resp.ok()).toBe(true);\n  const ct = resp.headers()['content-type'] ?? '';\n  // Service workers must be served as JavaScript\n  expect(ct).toMatch(/javascript/i);\n});\n\ntest('@smoke message area shows empty state placeholder', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.goto('/');\n  await expect(page.getByText('Your messages will appear here')).toBeVisible();\n});\n"
}
▶ VIEW RAW DATA

  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_01NbrC8MSVQrZLxt1fBNXcPU",
    "name": "Write",
    "input": {
      "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/smoke/smoke.spec.ts",
      "content": "/**\n * @smoke\n *\n * Smoke tests \u2014 fast sanity checks that the deployed app is reachable and\n * the critical shell elements are present.  These must pass before regression\n * tests are meaningful.\n */\n\nimport { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';\nimport { SPEECH_MOCK_SCRIPT } from '../helpers/speech-mock';\n\ntest.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.addInitScript(SPEECH_MOCK_SCRIPT);\n});\n\ntest('@smoke page loads with correct title', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.goto('/');\n  await expect(page).toHaveTitle('Hey Chill');\n});\n\ntest('@smoke app heading is \"Chill\"', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.goto('/');\n  await expect(page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Chill' })).toBeVisible();\n});\n\ntest('@smoke mic button is present and enabled', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.goto('/');\n  const micBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Start recording' });\n  await expect(micBtn).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(micBtn).toBeEnabled();\n});\n\ntest('@smoke settings button opens modal', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.goto('/');\n  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Settings' }).click();\n  await expect(page.getByRole('dialog')).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByText('Settings')).toBeVisible();\n});\n\ntest('@smoke manifest.json is reachable and valid', async ({ page }) => {\n  const resp = await page.request.get('/manifest.json');\n  expect(resp.ok()).toBe(true);\n  const manifest = await resp.json();\n  expect(manifest.name).toBe('Hey Chill');\n  expect(manifest.display).toBe('standalone');\n  expect(Array.isArray(manifest.icons)).toBe(true);\n  expect(manifest.icons.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n});\n\ntest('@smoke service worker is registered and active', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.goto('/');\n  // Allow SW to register\n  await page.waitForTimeout(1500);\n  const swState = await page.evaluate(async () => {\n    const regs = await navigator.serviceWorker.getRegistrations();\n    return regs.map((r) => ({\n      scope: r.scope,\n      state: r.active?.state ?? null,\n    }));\n  });\n  expect(swState.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n  expect(swState[0].state).toBe('activated');\n});\n\ntest('@smoke sw.js is reachable', async ({ page }) => {\n  const resp = await page.request.get('/sw.js');\n  expect(resp.ok()).toBe(true);\n  const ct = resp.headers()['content-type'] ?? '';\n  // Service workers must be served as JavaScript\n  expect(ct).toMatch(/javascript/i);\n});\n\ntest('@smoke message area shows empty state placeholder', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.goto('/');\n  await expect(page.getByText('Your messages will appear here')).toBeVisible();\n});\n"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/push-to-talk.spec.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * @regression\n *\n * Push-to-talk UI tests.\n * Covers button state transitions, ARIA attributes, hint text, and the\n * mic-ring animation class.\n */\n\nimport { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';\nimport { SPEECH_MOCK_SCRIPT, emitFinalResult, endRecognition } from '../helpers/speech-mock';\n\ntest.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.addInitScript(SPEECH_MOCK_SCRIPT);\n  await page.goto('/');\n});\n\ntest('@regression mic button has correct initial ARIA state', async ({ page }) => {\n  const micBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Start recording' });\n  await expect(micBtn).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false');\n  await expect(micBtn).toHaveAttribute('aria-label', 'Start recording');\n});\n\ntest('@regression mic hint initially reads \"Tap to speak\"', async ({ page }) => {\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-hint')).toHaveText('Tap to speak');\n});\n\ntest('@regression clicking mic button starts recording \u2014 UI updates', async ({ page }) => {\n  const micBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /recording/i });\n\n  // Click to start\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n\n  // aria-pressed should flip to true\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'true');\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-label', 'Stop recording');\n\n  // Hint text changes\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-hint')).toHaveText('Tap to stop');\n\n  // mic-ring and mic-btn get recording class\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-ring')).toHaveClass(/recording/);\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveClass(/recording/);\n\n  // Live transcript panel appears\n  await expect(page.locator('#live-transcript')).toBeVisible();\n});\n\ntest('@regression clicking mic button again stops recording \u2014 UI resets', async ({ page }) => {\n  // Start\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'true');\n\n  // Stop\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false');\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-label', 'Start recording');\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-hint')).toHaveText('Tap to speak');\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-ring')).not.toHaveClass(/recording/);\n  await expect(page.locator('#live-transcript')).toBeHidden();\n});\n\ntest('@regression live transcript shows \"Listening\u2026\" initially during recording', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await expect(page.locator('#live-text')).toHaveText('Listening\u2026');\n});\n\ntest('@regression not-supported banner hidden when SpeechRecognition is available', async ({ page }) => {\n  await expect(page.locator('#not-supported')).toBeHidden();\n});\n\ntest('@regression not-supported banner shown when SpeechRecognition unavailable', async ({ page: p2 }) => {\n  // New page without the mock \u2014 override to remove SpeechRecognition\n  await p2.addInitScript(`\n    delete window.SpeechRecognition;\n    delete window.webkitSpeechRecognition;\n  `);\n  await p2.goto('/');\n  await expect(p2.locator('#not-supported')).toBeVisible();\n  // Mic button should be disabled\n  await expect(p2.locator('#mic-btn')).toBeDisabled();\n});\n"
}

      "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/push-to-talk.spec.ts",
      "content": "/**\n * @regression\n *\n * Push-to-talk UI tests.\n * Covers button state transitions, ARIA attributes, hint text, and the\n * mic-ring animation class.\n */\n\nimport { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';\nimport { SPEECH_MOCK_SCRIPT, emitFinalResult, endRecognition } from '../helpers/speech-mock';\n\ntest.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.addInitScript(SPEECH_MOCK_SCRIPT);\n  await page.goto('/');\n});\n\ntest('@regression mic button has correct initial ARIA state', async ({ page }) => {\n  const micBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Start recording' });\n  await expect(micBtn).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false');\n  await expect(micBtn).toHaveAttribute('aria-label', 'Start recording');\n});\n\ntest('@regression mic hint initially reads \"Tap to speak\"', async ({ page }) => {\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-hint')).toHaveText('Tap to speak');\n});\n\ntest('@regression clicking mic button starts recording \u2014 UI updates', async ({ page }) => {\n  const micBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /recording/i });\n\n  // Click to start\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n\n  // aria-pressed should flip to true\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'true');\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-label', 'Stop recording');\n\n  // Hint text changes\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-hint')).toHaveText('Tap to stop');\n\n  // mic-ring and mic-btn get recording class\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-ring')).toHaveClass(/recording/);\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveClass(/recording/);\n\n  // Live transcript panel appears\n  await expect(page.locator('#live-transcript')).toBeVisible();\n});\n\ntest('@regression clicking mic button again stops recording \u2014 UI resets', async ({ page }) => {\n  // Start\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'true');\n\n  // Stop\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false');\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-label', 'Start recording');\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-hint')).toHaveText('Tap to speak');\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-ring')).not.toHaveClass(/recording/);\n  await expect(page.locator('#live-transcript')).toBeHidden();\n});\n\ntest('@regression live transcript shows \"Listening\u2026\" initially during recording', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await expect(page.locator('#live-text')).toHaveText('Listening\u2026');\n});\n\ntest('@regression not-supported banner hidden when SpeechRecognition is available', async ({ page }) => {\n  await expect(page.locator('#not-supported')).toBeHidden();\n});\n\ntest('@regression not-supported banner shown when SpeechRecognition unavailable', async ({ page: p2 }) => {\n  // New page without the mock \u2014 override to remove SpeechRecognition\n  await p2.addInitScript(`\n    delete window.SpeechRecognition;\n    delete window.webkitSpeechRecognition;\n  `);\n  await p2.goto('/');\n  await expect(p2.locator('#not-supported')).toBeVisible();\n  // Mic button should be disabled\n  await expect(p2.locator('#mic-btn')).toBeDisabled();\n});\n"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/transcription.spec.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * @regression\n *\n * Transcription display tests.\n * Verifies that interim results appear in the live transcript panel,\n * that final results accumulate, and that the transcript bar appears\n * after recording ends with final text.\n */\n\nimport { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';\nimport { SPEECH_MOCK_SCRIPT, emitInterimResult, emitFinalResult, endRecognition } from '../helpers/speech-mock';\n\ntest.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.addInitScript(SPEECH_MOCK_SCRIPT);\n  await page.goto('/');\n});\n\ntest('@regression interim transcript updates live-text while recording', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  // Wait for recognition to start\n  await page.waitForTimeout(100);\n\n  await emitInterimResult(page, 'Hello world');\n  await expect(page.locator('#live-text')).toHaveText('Hello world');\n});\n\ntest('@regression live-text updates as interim results arrive', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(100);\n\n  await emitInterimResult(page, 'Hello');\n  await expect(page.locator('#live-text')).toHaveText('Hello');\n\n  await emitInterimResult(page, 'Hello there');\n  await expect(page.locator('#live-text')).toHaveText('Hello there');\n});\n\ntest('@regression final transcript shown in transcript bar after stop', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(100);\n\n  // Emit a final result then stop\n  await emitFinalResult(page, 'Schedule a call for tomorrow');\n  await endRecognition(page);\n\n  // transcript-bar should appear with the final text\n  await expect(page.locator('#transcript-bar')).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.locator('#transcript-text')).toHaveText('Schedule a call for tomorrow');\n});\n\ntest('@regression transcript bar has Send and Discard buttons', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(100);\n  await emitFinalResult(page, 'Test message');\n  await endRecognition(page);\n\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Send' })).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Discard' })).toBeVisible();\n});\n\ntest('@regression discard hides transcript bar', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(100);\n  await emitFinalResult(page, 'Discard me');\n  await endRecognition(page);\n\n  await expect(page.locator('#transcript-bar')).toBeVisible();\n  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Discard' }).click();\n  await expect(page.locator('#transcript-bar')).toBeHidden();\n});\n\ntest('@regression transcript bar not shown when final transcript is empty', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(100);\n\n  // End recording without any final result\n  await endRecognition(page);\n\n  await expect(page.locator('#transcript-bar')).toBeHidden();\n});\n\ntest('@regression multiple final results are concatenated', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(100);\n\n  await emitFinalResult(page, 'Hello ');\n  await emitFinalResult(page, 'world');\n  await endRecognition(page);\n\n  await expect(page.locator('#transcript-text')).toHaveText(/Hello.*world/);\n});\n"
}

transcript bar appears\n * after recording ends with final text.\n */\n\nimport { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';\nimport { SPEECH_MOCK_SCRIPT, emitInterimResult, emitFinalResult, endRecognition } from '../helpers/speech-mock';\n\ntest.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.addInitScript(SPEECH_MOCK_SCRIPT);\n  await page.goto('/');\n});\n\ntest('@regression interim transcript updates live-text while recording', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  // Wait for recognition to start\n  await page.waitForTimeout(100);\n\n  await emitInterimResult(page, 'Hello world');\n  await expect(page.locator('#live-text')).toHaveText('Hello world');\n});\n\ntest('@regression live-text updates as interim results arrive', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(100);\n\n  await emitInterimResult(page, 'Hello');\n  await expect(page.locator('#live-text')).toHaveText('Hello');\n\n  await emitInterimResult(page, 'Hello there');\n  await expect(page.locator('#live-text')).toHaveText('Hello there');\n});\n\ntest('@regression final transcript shown in transcript bar after stop', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(100);\n\n  // Emit a final result then stop\n  await emitFinalResult(page, 'Schedule a call for tomorrow');\n  await endRecognition(page);\n\n  // transcript-bar should appear with the final text\n  await expect(page.locator('#transcript-bar')).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.locator('#transcript-text')).toHaveText('Schedule a call for tomorrow');\n});\n\ntest('@regression transcript bar has Send and Discard buttons', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(100);\n  await emitFinalResult(page, 'Test message');\n  await endRecognition(page);\n\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Send' })).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Discard' })).toBeVisible();\n});\n\ntest('@regression discard hides transcript bar', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(100);\n  await emitFinalResult(page, 'Discard me');\n  await endRecognition(page);\n\n  await expect(page.locator('#transcript-bar')).toBeVisible();\n  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Discard' }).click();\n  await expect(page.locator('#transcript-bar')).toBeHidden();\n});\n\ntest('@regression transcript bar not shown when final transcript is empty', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(100);\n\n  // End recording without any final result\n  await endRecognition(page);\n\n  await expect(page.locator('#transcript-bar')).toBeHidden();\n});\n\ntest('@regression multiple final results are concatenated', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(100);\n\n  await emitFinalResult(page, 'Hello ');\n  await emitFinalResult(page, 'world');\n  await endRecognition(page);\n\n  await expect(page.locator('#transcript-text')).toHaveText(/Hello.*world/);\n});\n"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #66
Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/vad.spec.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * @regression\n *\n * Voice Activity Detection (VAD) tests.\n * The app auto-stops after SILENCE_DELAY = 3500 ms of no speech results.\n *\n * Strategy: use fake timers (page.clock.install) so we can advance time\n * without actually waiting 3.5 seconds.\n */\n\nimport { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';\nimport { SPEECH_MOCK_SCRIPT, emitFinalResult } from '../helpers/speech-mock';\n\ntest.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.addInitScript(SPEECH_MOCK_SCRIPT);\n});\n\ntest('@regression VAD auto-stops recording after 3500 ms of silence', async ({ page }) => {\n  // Install fake clock before page scripts run\n  await page.clock.install({ time: 0 });\n  await page.goto('/');\n\n  // Start recording\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(50); // let start event fire\n\n  // Verify recording is active\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'true');\n\n  // Advance time past the 3500 ms silence threshold\n  await page.clock.fastForward(3600);\n\n  // Give async events time to fire\n  await page.waitForTimeout(100);\n\n  // Recording should have stopped\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false');\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-hint')).toHaveText('Tap to speak');\n});\n\ntest('@regression speech result resets the silence timer', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.clock.install({ time: 0 });\n  await page.goto('/');\n\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(50);\n\n  // Advance 2000 ms \u2014 not yet at threshold\n  await page.clock.fastForward(2000);\n  await page.waitForTimeout(50);\n\n  // Emit a result \u2014 this should reset the 3500 ms timer\n  await emitFinalResult(page, 'Keep going');\n\n  // Advance another 2000 ms \u2014 total 4000 ms, but timer was reset at 2000 ms\n  // so actual elapsed since reset is 2000 ms (< 3500 ms)\n  await page.clock.fastForward(2000);\n  await page.waitForTimeout(50);\n\n  // Should still be recording\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'true');\n});\n\ntest('@regression VAD fires after reset + another 3500 ms of silence', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.clock.install({ time: 0 });\n  await page.goto('/');\n\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(50);\n\n  // Emit result at 2000 ms to reset timer\n  await page.clock.fastForward(2000);\n  await emitFinalResult(page, 'Reset');\n\n  // Now advance 3600 ms more past the reset point\n  await page.clock.fastForward(3600);\n  await page.waitForTimeout(100);\n\n  // Should have auto-stopped\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false');\n});\n\ntest('@regression SILENCE_DELAY constant is 3500 ms', async ({ page }) => {\n  // Verify the constant value is what the spec requires (read from app.js)\n  const resp = await page.request.get('/app.js');\n  const src = await resp.text();\n  expect(src).toContain('SILENCE_DELAY = 3500');\n});\n"
}
▶ VIEW RAW DATA

  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_013DSMhd2B9xxbeTLHVF3Jw1",
    "name": "Write",
    "input": {
      "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/vad.spec.ts",
      "content": "/**\n * @regression\n *\n * Voice Activity Detection (VAD) tests.\n * The app auto-stops after SILENCE_DELAY = 3500 ms of no speech results.\n *\n * Strategy: use fake timers (page.clock.install) so we can advance time\n * without actually waiting 3.5 seconds.\n */\n\nimport { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';\nimport { SPEECH_MOCK_SCRIPT, emitFinalResult } from '../helpers/speech-mock';\n\ntest.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.addInitScript(SPEECH_MOCK_SCRIPT);\n});\n\ntest('@regression VAD auto-stops recording after 3500 ms of silence', async ({ page }) => {\n  // Install fake clock before page scripts run\n  await page.clock.install({ time: 0 });\n  await page.goto('/');\n\n  // Start recording\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(50); // let start event fire\n\n  // Verify recording is active\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'true');\n\n  // Advance time past the 3500 ms silence threshold\n  await page.clock.fastForward(3600);\n\n  // Give async events time to fire\n  await page.waitForTimeout(100);\n\n  // Recording should have stopped\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false');\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-hint')).toHaveText('Tap to speak');\n});\n\ntest('@regression speech result resets the silence timer', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.clock.install({ time: 0 });\n  await page.goto('/');\n\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(50);\n\n  // Advance 2000 ms \u2014 not yet at threshold\n  await page.clock.fastForward(2000);\n  await page.waitForTimeout(50);\n\n  // Emit a result \u2014 this should reset the 3500 ms timer\n  await emitFinalResult(page, 'Keep going');\n\n  // Advance another 2000 ms \u2014 total 4000 ms, but timer was reset at 2000 ms\n  // so actual elapsed since reset is 2000 ms (< 3500 ms)\n  await page.clock.fastForward(2000);\n  await page.waitForTimeout(50);\n\n  // Should still be recording\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'true');\n});\n\ntest('@regression VAD fires after reset + another 3500 ms of silence', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.clock.install({ time: 0 });\n  await page.goto('/');\n\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(50);\n\n  // Emit result at 2000 ms to reset timer\n  await page.clock.fastForward(2000);\n  await emitFinalResult(page, 'Reset');\n\n  // Now advance 3600 ms more past the reset point\n  await page.clock.fastForward(3600);\n  await page.waitForTimeout(100);\n\n  // Should have auto-stopped\n  await expect(page.locator('#mic-btn')).toHaveAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false');\n});\n\ntest('@regression SILENCE_DELAY constant is 3500 ms', async ({ page }) => {\n  // Verify the constant value is what the spec requires (read from app.js)\n  const resp = await page.request.get('/app.js');\n  const src = await resp.text();\n  expect(src).toContain('SILENCE_DELAY = 3500');\n});\n"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #68
Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/regression/webhook.spec.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * @regression\n *\n * Slack webhook send tests.\n * Uses Playwright network interception to verify the POST is made to the\n * configured webhook URL with the correct JSON payload shape, without\n * hitting a real webhook endpoint.\n */\n\nimport { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';\nimport { SPEECH_MOCK_SCRIPT, emitFinalResult, endRecognition } from '../helpers/speech-mock';\n\nconst MOCK_WEBHOOK = 'https://mock-webhook.example.com/hook';\n\nasync function setupWithWebhook(page: import('@playwright/test').Page) {\n  await page.addInitScript(SPEECH_MOCK_SCRIPT);\n  // Pre-seed localStorage with webhook URL before page scripts read it\n  await page.addInitScript((url) => {\n    localStorage.setItem('webhookUrl', url);\n  }, MOCK_WEBHOOK);\n  await page.goto('/');\n}\n\nasync function recordAndSend(page: import('@playwright/test').Page, transcript: string) {\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(100);\n  await emitFinalResult(page, transcript);\n  await endRecognition(page);\n  await expect(page.locator('#transcript-bar')).toBeVisible();\n  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Send' }).click();\n}\n\ntest('@regression webhook POST is made to configured URL', async ({ page }) => {\n  let capturedRequest: import('@playwright/test').Request | null = null;\n\n  await page.route(MOCK_WEBHOOK, (route, request) => {\n    capturedRequest = request;\n    route.fulfill({ status: 200, body: 'ok' });\n  });\n\n  await setupWithWebhook(page);\n  await recordAndSend(page, 'Call Chill now');\n\n  expect(capturedRequest).not.toBeNull();\n  expect(capturedRequest!.method()).toBe('POST');\n});\n\ntest('@regression webhook payload contains correct fields', async ({ page }) => {\n  let payload: Record<string, unknown> | null = null;\n\n  await page.route(MOCK_WEBHOOK, async (route, request) => {\n    payload = JSON.parse(request.postData() ?? '{}');\n    await route.fulfill({ status: 200, body: 'ok' });\n  });\n\n  await setupWithWebhook(page);\n  const transcript = 'Hey Chill, what is the weather?';\n  await recordAndSend(page, transcript);\n\n  expect(payload).not.toBeNull();\n  expect(payload!.text).toBe(transcript);\n  expect(payload!.source).toBe('hey-chill');\n  expect(typeof payload!.timestamp).toBe('string');\n  // timestamp should be an ISO-8601 date string\n  expect(new Date(payload!.timestamp as string).toISOString()).toBe(payload!.timestamp);\n});\n\ntest('@regression webhook Content-Type header is application/json', async ({ page }) => {\n  let contentType: string | null = null;\n\n  await page.route(MOCK_WEBHOOK, async (route, request) => {\n    contentType = request.headers()['content-type'] ?? null;\n    await route.fulfill({ status: 200, body: 'ok' });\n  });\n\n  await setupWithWebhook(page);\n  await recordAndSend(page, 'Test payload');\n\n  expect(contentType).not.toBeNull();\n  expect(contentType!.toLowerCase()).toContain('application/json');\n});\n\ntest('@regression message status shows \"Sent\" on 200 response', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.route(MOCK_WEBHOOK, (route) => {\n    route.fulfill({ status: 200, body: 'ok' });\n  });\n\n  await setupWithWebhook(page);\n  await recordAndSend(page, 'Sent message test');\n\n  // Message card should appear in history with \"Sent\" status\n  await expect(page.locator('.message.sent .message-status')).toHaveText('Sent');\n});\n\ntest('@regression message status shows \"Failed\" on non-200 response', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.route(MOCK_WEBHOOK, (route) => {\n    route.fulfill({ status: 500, body: 'error' });\n  });\n\n  await setupWithWebhook(page);\n  await recordAndSend(page, 'Failed message test');\n\n  await expect(page.locator('.message.error .message-status')).toHaveText('Failed');\n});\n\ntest('@regression message status shows \"No webhook\" when no URL configured', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.addInitScript(SPEECH_MOCK_SCRIPT);\n  // Clear webhook URL\n  await page.addInitScript(() => {\n    localStorage.removeItem('webhookUrl');\n  });\n  await page.goto('/');\n\n  await recordAndSend(page, 'No webhook test');\n\n  await expect(page.locator('.message.no-webhook .message-status')).toHaveText('No webhook set');\n});\n\ntest('@regression message text appears in history immediately', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.route(MOCK_WEBHOOK, (route) => route.fulfill({ status: 200 }));\n  await setupWithWebhook(page);\n\n  const text = 'Quick message';\n  await recordAndSend(page, text);\n\n  await expect(page.locator('.message .message-text')).toHaveText(text);\n  // Empty state should be hidden\n  await expect(page.locator('#messages-empty')).toBeHidden();\n});\n\ntest('@regression message text is HTML-escaped to prevent XSS', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.route(MOCK_WEBHOOK, (route) => route.fulfill({ status: 200 }));\n  await setupWithWebhook(page);\n\n  // Manually set transcript text to XSS payload and click send\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(100);\n  await emitFinalResult(page, '<script>alert(1)</script>');\n  await endRecognition(page);\n  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Send' }).click();\n\n  // The raw text should not have created a script element\n  const scriptCount = await page.locator('.message script').count();\n  expect(scriptCount).toBe(0);\n\n  // The escaped text should appear as literal characters\n  const msgText = await page.locator('.message .message-text').textContent();\n  expect(msgText).toContain('<script>');\n});\n"
}

      "content": "/**\n * @regression\n *\n * Slack webhook send tests.\n * Uses Playwright network interception to verify the POST is made to the\n * configured webhook URL with the correct JSON payload shape, without\n * hitting a real webhook endpoint.\n */\n\nimport { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';\nimport { SPEECH_MOCK_SCRIPT, emitFinalResult, endRecognition } from '../helpers/speech-mock';\n\nconst MOCK_WEBHOOK = 'https://mock-webhook.example.com/hook';\n\nasync function setupWithWebhook(page: import('@playwright/test').Page) {\n  await page.addInitScript(SPEECH_MOCK_SCRIPT);\n  // Pre-seed localStorage with webhook URL before page scripts read it\n  await page.addInitScript((url) => {\n    localStorage.setItem('webhookUrl', url);\n  }, MOCK_WEBHOOK);\n  await page.goto('/');\n}\n\nasync function recordAndSend(page: import('@playwright/test').Page, transcript: string) {\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(100);\n  await emitFinalResult(page, transcript);\n  await endRecognition(page);\n  await expect(page.locator('#transcript-bar')).toBeVisible();\n  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Send' }).click();\n}\n\ntest('@regression webhook POST is made to configured URL', async ({ page }) => {\n  let capturedRequest: import('@playwright/test').Request | null = null;\n\n  await page.route(MOCK_WEBHOOK, (route, request) => {\n    capturedRequest = request;\n    route.fulfill({ status: 200, body: 'ok' });\n  });\n\n  await setupWithWebhook(page);\n  await recordAndSend(page, 'Call Chill now');\n\n  expect(capturedRequest).not.toBeNull();\n  expect(capturedRequest!.method()).toBe('POST');\n});\n\ntest('@regression webhook payload contains correct fields', async ({ page }) => {\n  let payload: Record<string, unknown> | null = null;\n\n  await page.route(MOCK_WEBHOOK, async (route, request) => {\n    payload = JSON.parse(request.postData() ?? '{}');\n    await route.fulfill({ status: 200, body: 'ok' });\n  });\n\n  await setupWithWebhook(page);\n  const transcript = 'Hey Chill, what is the weather?';\n  await recordAndSend(page, transcript);\n\n  expect(payload).not.toBeNull();\n  expect(payload!.text).toBe(transcript);\n  expect(payload!.source).toBe('hey-chill');\n  expect(typeof payload!.timestamp).toBe('string');\n  // timestamp should be an ISO-8601 date string\n  expect(new Date(payload!.timestamp as string).toISOString()).toBe(payload!.timestamp);\n});\n\ntest('@regression webhook Content-Type header is application/json', async ({ page }) => {\n  let contentType: string | null = null;\n\n  await page.route(MOCK_WEBHOOK, async (route, request) => {\n    contentType = request.headers()['content-type'] ?? null;\n    await route.fulfill({ status: 200, body: 'ok' });\n  });\n\n  await setupWithWebhook(page);\n  await recordAndSend(page, 'Test payload');\n\n  expect(contentType).not.toBeNull();\n  expect(contentType!.toLowerCase()).toContain('application/json');\n});\n\ntest('@regression message status shows \"Sent\" on 200 response', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.route(MOCK_WEBHOOK, (route) => {\n    route.fulfill({ status: 200, body: 'ok' });\n  });\n\n  await setupWithWebhook(page);\n  await recordAndSend(page, 'Sent message test');\n\n  // Message card should appear in history with \"Sent\" status\n  await expect(page.locator('.message.sent .message-status')).toHaveText('Sent');\n});\n\ntest('@regression message status shows \"Failed\" on non-200 response', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.route(MOCK_WEBHOOK, (route) => {\n    route.fulfill({ status: 500, body: 'error' });\n  });\n\n  await setupWithWebhook(page);\n  await recordAndSend(page, 'Failed message test');\n\n  await expect(page.locator('.message.error .message-status')).toHaveText('Failed');\n});\n\ntest('@regression message status shows \"No webhook\" when no URL configured', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.addInitScript(SPEECH_MOCK_SCRIPT);\n  // Clear webhook URL\n  await page.addInitScript(() => {\n    localStorage.removeItem('webhookUrl');\n  });\n  await page.goto('/');\n\n  await recordAndSend(page, 'No webhook test');\n\n  await expect(page.locator('.message.no-webhook .message-status')).toHaveText('No webhook set');\n});\n\ntest('@regression message text appears in history immediately', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.route(MOCK_WEBHOOK, (route) => route.fulfill({ status: 200 }));\n  await setupWithWebhook(page);\n\n  const text = 'Quick message';\n  await recordAndSend(page, text);\n\n  await expect(page.locator('.message .message-text')).toHaveText(text);\n  // Empty state should be hidden\n  await expect(page.locator('#messages-empty')).toBeHidden();\n});\n\ntest('@regression message text is HTML-escaped to prevent XSS', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.route(MOCK_WEBHOOK, (route) => route.fulfill({ status: 200 }));\n  await setupWithWebhook(page);\n\n  // Manually set transcript text to XSS payload and click send\n  await page.locator('#mic-btn').click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(100);\n  await emitFinalResult(page, '<script>alert(1)</script>');\n  await endRecognition(page);\n  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Send' }).click();\n\n  // The raw text should not have created a script element\n  const scriptCount = await page.locator('.message script').count();\n  expect(scriptCount).toBe(0);\n\n  // The escaped text should appear as literal characters\n  const msgText = await page.locator('.message .message-text').textContent();\n  expect(msgText).toContain('<script>');\n});\n"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}
